server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acds_client_data_t
Per-client connection data. struct migration_context_t
In-memory host migration context. struct acds_server_t
Discovery server state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 892 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

892                                      {
893   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
894   if (!ctx) {
895     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
896     return NULL;
897   }
898 
899   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
900   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
901 
902   // Get client IP for logging
903   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
904   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
905 
906   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
907 
908   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
909   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
910   if (!client_data) {
911     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
912     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
913     return NULL;
914   }
915   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
916   client_data->joined_session = false;
917   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
918 
919   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
920   char crypto_name[96];
921   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
922   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
923   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
924     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
925     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
926     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
927     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
928     return NULL;
929   }
930 
931   // Set server identity keys for handshake
932   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
933   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
934   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
935   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
936 
937   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
938     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
939     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
940     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
941     return NULL;
942   }
943 
944   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
945             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
946 
947   // Create transport for handshake and subsequent client communication
948   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
949   if (!transport) {
950     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
951     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
952     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
953     return NULL;
954   }
955 
956   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
957   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
958 
959   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
960   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
961   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
962     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
963     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
964     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
965     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
966     return NULL;
967   }
968 
969   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
970   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
971   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
972     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
973     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
974     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
975     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
976     return NULL;
977   }
978 
979   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
980   {
981     packet_type_t packet_type;
982     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
983     size_t payload_len = 0;
984     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
985     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
986       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
987       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
988       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
989       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
990       return NULL;
991     }
992     handshake_result =
993         crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
994     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
995   }
996   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
997     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
998     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
999     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1000     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1001     return NULL;
1002   }
1003 
1004   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
1005   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1006     packet_type_t packet_type = 0;
1007     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
1008     size_t payload_len = 0;
1009     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
1010     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1011       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
1012       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1013       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1014       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1015       return NULL;
1016     }
1017     handshake_result =
1018         crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
1019     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1020     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1021       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
1022       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1023       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1024       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1025       return NULL;
1026     }
1027   } else {
1028     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
1029   }
1030 
1031   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1032   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
1033 
1034   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
1035   client_data->transport = transport;
1036   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
1037   transport->user_data = client_data;
1038 
1039   // Main packet processing loop
1040   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1041     packet_type_t packet_type;
1042     void *payload = NULL;
1043     size_t payload_size = 0;
1044 
1045     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1046     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1047     if (result < 0) {
1048       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1049       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1050       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1051 
1052       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1053       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1054       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1055           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1056         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
1057         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1058         if (payload) {
1059           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1060         }
1061         continue;
1062       }
1063 
1064       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1065       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1066       if (payload) {
1067         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1068       }
1069       break;
1070     }
1071 
1072     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1073 
1074     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1075     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1076       bool allowed =
1077           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1078 
1079       if (!allowed) {
1080         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1081                  "allowed",
1082                  client_ip, packet_type);
1083 
1084         // Send error response using persistent transport
1085         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1086                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1087 
1088         // Free payload and continue
1089         if (payload) {
1090           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1091         }
1092         continue;
1093       }
1094     }
1095 
1096     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1097     // Set server context for callbacks
1098     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1099     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1100 
1101     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1102         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1103 
1104     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1105       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1106                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1107     }
1108 
1109     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1110     if (payload) {
1111       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1112     }
1113 
1114     // Keep handler running after session_established
1115     // Handler continues to receive and relay WebRTC signaling packets (SDP, ICE)
1116     // Broadcasts don't go through this socket - they use tcp_server_foreach_client() to write directly
1117   }
1118 
1119   // Cleanup - handler always cleans up since it runs until connection closes
1120   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1121   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1122 
1123   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1124   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1125   socket_close(client_socket);
1126 
1127   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1128 
1129   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1130   return NULL;
1131 }

    acip_send_error(), acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,

    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,

    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),

    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,

    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_reject_client(), tcp_server_remove_client(),

    public_key_t::type, acip_transport::user_data, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 191 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


191                                                                                        {
192   if (!server || !config) {
193     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
194   }
195 
196   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
197   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
198 
199   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
200   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
201   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
202     return result;
203   }
204 
205   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
206   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
207   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
208     database_close(server->db);
209     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
210   }
211 
212   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
213   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
214 
215   // Configure TCP server
216   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
217       .port = config->port,
218       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
219       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
220       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
221       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
222       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
223       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
224       .user_data = server,
225   };
226 
227   // Initialize TCP server
228   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
229   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
230     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
231     database_close(server->db);
232     return result;
233   }
234 
235   // Initialize background worker thread pool
236   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
237   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
238   if (!server->worker_pool) {
239     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
240     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
241     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
242     database_close(server->db);
243     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
244   }
245 
246   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
247   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
248     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
249   }
250 
251   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
252   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),

    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,

    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 255 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


255                                                          {
256   if (!server) {
257     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
258   }
259 
260   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
261 
262   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
263   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
264 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 266 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


266                                                  {
267   if (!server) {
268     return;
269   }
270 
271   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
272   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
273 
274   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
275   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
276 
277   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
278   size_t remaining_clients;
279   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
280   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
281 
282   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
283          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
284     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
285               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
286     platform_sleep_ms(100);
287     shutdown_attempts++;
288   }
289 
290   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
291     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
292   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
293     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
294   }
295 
296   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
297   if (server->worker_pool) {
298     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
299     server->worker_pool = NULL;
300     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
301   }
302 
303   // Destroy rate limiter
304   if (server->rate_limiter) {
305     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
306     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
307   }
308 
309   // Close database
310   if (server->db) {
311     database_close(server->db);
312     server->db = NULL;
313   }
314 
315   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
316 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1137 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1137                                                {
1138   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1139   if (!ctx) {
1140     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1141     return NULL;
1142   }
1143 
1144   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1145   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1146   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1147 
1148   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1149 
1150   // Allocate client_data
1151   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1152   if (!client_data) {
1153     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1154     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1155     return NULL;
1156   }
1157 
1158   client_data->transport = transport;
1159   client_data->joined_session = false;
1160   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1161   transport->user_data = client_data;
1162 
1163   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1164   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1165   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1166   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1167 
1168   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1169   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1170     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1171     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1172     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1173     return NULL;
1174   }
1175 
1176   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1177             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1178 
1179   // Determine if client authentication is required by checking server configuration
1180   bool auth_required = server->config.require_client_identity;
1181   ctx->auth_required = auth_required;
1182 
1183   // Skip crypto handshake only for wss:// connections WITHOUT authentication requirement
1184   // For plain ws://, always do crypto handshake
1185   // For wss:// WITH auth requirement, always do crypto handshake
1186   bool skip_handshake = ctx->is_secure && !auth_required;
1187 
1188   if (skip_handshake) {
1189     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1190     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption used, no auth required)",
1191              client_ip);
1192   } else {
1193     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - proceeding with crypto handshake%s", client_ip,
1194              auth_required ? " (client authentication required)" : "");
1195   }
1196 
1197   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1198   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1199     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1200     size_t recv_len = 0;
1201     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1202 
1203     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1204     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1205       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1206       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1207         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1208         if (alloc_buffer) {
1209           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1210         }
1211         continue;
1212       }
1213 
1214       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1215       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1216       if (alloc_buffer) {
1217         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1218       }
1219       break;
1220     }
1221 
1222     // Parse packet header from received data
1223     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1224       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1225       if (alloc_buffer) {
1226         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1227       }
1228       continue;
1229     }
1230 
1231     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1232     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1233     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1234     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1235 
1236     // Validate payload size
1237     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1238       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1239                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1240       if (alloc_buffer) {
1241         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1242       }
1243       continue;
1244     }
1245 
1246     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1247 
1248     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1249     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1250       bool allowed =
1251           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1252 
1253       if (!allowed) {
1254         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1255                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1256                  client_ip, packet_type);
1257         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1258                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1259         if (alloc_buffer) {
1260           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1261         }
1262         continue;
1263       }
1264     }
1265 
1266     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1267     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1268     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1269 
1270     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1271         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1272 
1273     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1274       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1275                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1276     }
1277 
1278     if (alloc_buffer) {
1279       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1280     }
1281   }
1282 
1283   // Cleanup
1284   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1285   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1286 
1287   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1288 
1289   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1290 
1291   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1292 
1293   return NULL;
1294 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_client_context_t::auth_required, buffer_pool_free(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    websocket_client_context_t::is_secure,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    acds_config_t::require_client_identity, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_remove_client(), acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().

    Version 0.9.13
    ascii-chat
